params.name: Epic of Gilgamesh
params.source: Dragon Chest (5%), Fragment Crafting (4.1%), Grandmaster Scrolls (1.5%), Lottery Machine (0.18%), Wishing (0.15%)
params.description:
On May 6, 1850 AD, this epic poem was found in the tomb of Ashurbanipal in Nineveh, the ancient capital of Assyria.
More than 3000 years ago, the Epic of Gilgamesh was written on 11 clay slabs with a total of 3600 lines. It tells the story of the friendship between King Gilgamesh of Uruk and his friend Enkidu. One of the famous parts of the poem tells the story of Ea, the water god, who wants to destroy mankind with a flood. This story is also known as Mesopotamian Noah's Ark.

[Excerpts]
